bullets.text: Annual CR 92.4; net income $1B; NPW ~$14.5B (+3.4%); over 750,000 net new policyholders; Direct finished at 94.6 CR despite three months exceeding 96 — new/renewal cohorts met lifetime targets.

$1 extraordinary dividend paid December plus $0.40 variable dividend (Gainshare 1.50); $223M hybrid debt retired at 95 cents on dollar; 13.3M shares repurchased; debt-to-capital 24.5%; portfolio total return 7.8% contributing $765.7M to comprehensive income.

Snapshot redesigned and relaunched — consumer experience simplified, usage integrated into mainstream product, marketing message simplified, costs reduced; 2011 plan calls for more states, improved customer experience, and active marketing campaign.

Agency auto approaching all-time high PIF; Direct PIFs grew strongly; approaching 1M customers with multiple products — significant and growing percentage bundling homeowners or renters with auto; policy life expectancy at all-time highs in both channels.

Name Your Price contributed notably to 2010 growth; unified Agency/Direct product design completed after multi-year effort; Household View transitioning customer service from policy-entity to household-entity to drive retention.

Flo with 2.5M Facebook fans — no insurance comparables; 'Messenger' character developed and tested in late 2010 for 2011 launch; Jeff Charney hired as Chief Marketing Officer; surveyed as company 'most positively changing how auto insurance is done.'

All 51 regulatory jurisdictions profitable except 4; full-year profitability maintained vs. 96 target throughout decade — never failed to meet or exceed CR objective; PIP states largely resolved and positioned for growth in 2011.

Bodily injury severity declining — unusual direction not easily reconciled with experience; frequency manageable; PIP states improving results; Commercial Auto facing employment-driven demand weakness rather than loss cost issues.

'Destination Company' evolution: from transaction to customer-care culture; policy life expectancy at all-time highs in both channels; rate stability at renewal commitment reinforcing long-term customer relationships.

J.D. Power ranking improved for 7th consecutive year; named top-rated carrier by independent agents in J.D. Power Agency Satisfaction Study — superior in multiple specific dimensions; NPS continuing to improve with room to grow.
